events.title: US Navy Blockade of Strait of Hormuz Ordered
events.desc: President Donald Trump has ordered a US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz following the failure of peace talks with Iran. This escalation signals a significant increase in tensions and potential for confrontation in a critical global shipping lane. The blockade aims to pressure Iran by restricting its maritime trade and access to vital resources.

events.title: US Warships Transit Strait of Hormuz for Mine Clearance
events.desc: US Navy warships have transited the Strait of Hormuz to commence clearing Iranian-laid mines. This operation, confirmed by US Central Command, comes amidst heightened tensions and denial from Iran's Revolutionary Guards. The transit marks a significant step in securing the vital waterway.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Kill Infant in Southern Lebanon During Funeral
events.desc: An Israeli strike in southern Lebanon has resulted in the death of an infant girl and other relatives during her father's funeral. This incident highlights the ongoing civilian toll of the conflict and raises serious concerns about adherence to ceasefire agreements and international humanitarian law.

events.title: Israeli Minister Visits Al-Aqsa Mosque Compound
events.desc: Israel's far-right National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir visited the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound, a highly sensitive site in Jerusalem. His visit, aimed at increasing access for Jewish worshippers, has drawn strong condemnation from Palestinians and Jordan, escalating religious and political tensions in the region.

events.title: Intensive Israeli Strikes Across Lebanon Post-Ceasefire Announcement
events.desc: Despite a US-Iran ceasefire announcement, Israel has conducted significant strikes across Lebanon, hitting areas including Beirut's southern suburbs, southern Lebanon, and the Bekaa Valley. This has led to numerous casualties and prompted Iran to label the actions a 'grave violation' of the ceasefire.

events.title: Iran's Asymmetric Counterair Campaign Continues
events.desc: Recent Iranian drone and missile attacks on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia highlight a deliberate counterair campaign. The strikes, which damaged critical assets like the E-3 Sentry and KC-135 tankers, demonstrate Iran's asymmetric strategy against US and Israeli air power in the region.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: US Deploys Mines over Iranian Village
events.desc: Evidence suggests the US may have deployed Gator Scatterable Mines over Kafari, a village near Shiraz, Iran. Iranian media reported fatalities, and experts identified the munitions as US-made. This alleged use of mines marks a serious escalation in the conflict.

events.title: Iran Imposes Near-Total Internet Blackout During Strikes
events.desc: During recent strikes by adversaries, the Iranian government deliberately crippled its communications infrastructure, causing a 98% collapse in internet traffic. This near-total blackout was imposed on the Iranian population, distinct from the military targets of the external strikes.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Nearly 800 Hungarian government email addresses and passwords have been leaked online, exposing vulnerabilities in ministries handling sensitive information. This breach affects 12 out of 13 ministries and raises significant security concerns regarding state data protection.

events.title: AI Used to Amplify Hate Speech in India
events.desc: India's ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is reportedly using Artificial Intelligence to amplify hate speech, particularly in states bordering Bangladesh. AI-generated videos and captions promote divisive narratives, raising concerns about the spread of disinformation and targeted incitement.

events.title: Houthi Missile Attack Underscores Regional Pressure
events.desc: The Houthis have fired a missile, serving as a stark reminder of the ongoing pressure in the region. This action underscores the complex web of proxy actions and their impact on regional stability and maritime security.

events.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Conclude Without Agreement
events.desc: High-stakes peace talks between the United States and Iran in Islamabad have concluded without reaching an agreement. Despite reported progress on some points, significant disagreements, particularly concerning Iran's nuclear program, remain, prolonging uncertainty.

events.title: Lebanon and Israel Officials to Meet Amidst Tensions
events.desc: Officials from Lebanon and Israel are scheduled to meet in the US. The timing of these talks, occurring amid ongoing conflict and differing interpretations of ceasefire agreements, underscores the delicate diplomatic efforts required to de-escalate regional tensions.

events.title: Palestinians Condemn Israeli Minister's Al-Aqsa Visit
events.desc: Palestinians have strongly condemned the visit of Israel's far-right security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, to the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound. The act is viewed as a provocation that further inflames religious and political sensitivities in the already volatile region.

events.title: Stampede at Haiti Fortress Kills Dozens
events.desc: A stampede at Haiti's historic Citadelle Laferriere, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, has resulted in the deaths of at least 30 people. The incident occurred during a crowded annual celebration, highlighting safety concerns at major public gatherings.
